name: heavy_payload_burst
description: Few messages but heavy. Each m_workersQueue element is a std::vector<char> ~32 KB (capped by the agent→manager wire-protocol limit, see notes); a moderate backlog still weighs hundreds of MB. Validates that OOM risk doesn't require 'many' messages — heavy ones are enough.
notes: Payload is inflated to 32 KB via load.payload_size_bytes (the value of file.path is extended; wazuh-states-fim-files has dynamic:strict so a new top-level field would be rejected with HTTP 400).

Hard ceiling on payload_size_bytes: remoted's frame buffer is OS_MAXSTR=65536 bytes (shared/include/defs.h:51). Going above ~50 KB triggers WARNING (2202) 'Error uncompressing string' + 'Decrypt the message fail' and the manager closes the agent connection BEFORE the message reaches m_workersQueue. The first heavy_payload_burst attempt at 1 MB hit exactly this: 100 sessions, 100 failed, RSS flat at 27.9 MB (see results_heavy_payload_burst).

Hypothesis at the new size: 150 agents × 300 DataValue = 45000 messages × 32 KB = ~1.4 GB if the queue retains all in-flight elements. Lower bound for OOM observation, still well above the baseline RSS.
Proxy: monitor_rss_mb.png + monitor_vms_mb.png — RSS peaks correlated with Start bursts, not with duration.
Expect end_ack_error if the indexer HTTP response returns 413 (inherited from the large_payload scenario).
